mappings.rationaleAI: The nervous system covers neurons, synaptic transmission, central and peripheral nervous systems, and the autonomic nervous system -- nervous and sensory tissues that form a specialized organ system relating to the environment.

Hormones and the endocrine system (insulin, adrenaline, thyroid) represent endocrine tissues and the endocrine system as a specialized organ system, with feedback loops illustrating functional interdependence.
The evolution of lungs from aquatic to terrestrial breathing covers the respiratory system as a specialized organ system serving cell metabolism, including the diaphragm and ventilation mechanics.

The heart and Harvey's discovery of blood circulation directly address the circulatory system as a specialized organ system, and the interrelationship between circulatory and respiratory systems illustrates functional interdependence.

The evolution of teeth from dermal denticles to differentiated mammalian dentition illustrates skeletal derivatives and the relationship between form (tooth morphology) and function (diet adaptation).
Cephalopod biology including complex nervous systems, chromatophores, camera-like eyes, and three hearts illustrates diverse animal tissue organization and specialized organ systems in invertebrates.
Photosynthesis in vascular plants involves chloroplasts in leaf tissue and the physiology of plant organs, directly relevant to the physiology of organs in vascular plants.

Bird migration involves navigational mechanisms (sensory systems), physiological adaptations (fat storage, flight endurance), and coordination between muscular and nervous systems, illustrating interrelationships between organ systems.
Cell biology including cell division (mitosis, meiosis) and stem cells relates to the development of organ systems and the cellular basis of tissue differentiation.
